priority.title: SEC filing: 8-K — Fermi Inc. (CURRENT REPORT)
priority.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. has issued $431.25 million in aggregate principal amount of 5.00% convertible senior notes due 2031, with total net proceeds of approximately $416.81 million, which will be used for general corporate purposes. The notes are convertible into cash, shares of common stock, or a combination thereof, at the company's election, with an initial conversion price of approximately $9.52 per share. The company also entered into capped call transactions to reduce potential dilution to its common stock upon conversion of the notes, using approximately $34.5 million of the net proceeds to pay for these transactions.

priority.title: SEC filing: 8-K — Fermi Inc. (CURRENT REPORT)
priority.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. discloses the immediate resignation of director Miles Everson, effective July 10, 2026, due to a disagreement over access to company books and records and the delegation of financing transaction oversight to the Finance Committee. Everson's resignation was submitted in a letter, which is attached to the filing as an exhibit, and he did not serve on any committee of the Board at the time of his resignation. The company believes Everson was granted full access to its books and records, except for certain litigation-related materials, and had previously approved the establishment of the Finance Committee.

priority.title: SEC filing: 8-K — Fermi Inc. (CURRENT REPORT)
priority.formExplanation: A current report — the form companies use to disclose material events between quarters (deals, leadership changes, results, financings).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc. commenced an offering for the sale of $350 million aggregate principal amount of convertible senior notes due 2031 to qualified institutional buyers, with the option to purchase an additional $52.5 million. The company intends to use the net proceeds to pay for capped call transactions and for general corporate purposes. Fermi Inc. also disclosed that it has deferred its election to be taxed as a real estate investment trust, and now expects to make this election for its taxable year ending December 31, 2026.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, Vicksburg Investments Management LLC, and other parties have withdrawn their solicitation to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders due to an untenable timeline caused by a judge's recusal in pending litigation. The special meeting was intended to address entrenching actions taken by the company's directors, but the parties will not act on any agent designations received from shareholders unless the solicitation is resumed. The withdrawal comes after the parties had filed a definitive proxy statement and accompanying agent designation card with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has suspended his current proxy campaign to call a special meeting of shareholders due to a judge's last-minute recusal, which has disrupted the timeline for the strategic process. Neugebauer's campaign had earned the backing of over 70% of votes cast and two leading proxy advisory firms, and he remains confident in Fermi's ability to secure a tenant group and achieve long-term success. Neugebauer will continue to push for good governance through the judicial process and monitor the board's execution on Project Matador to ensure maximum value for stakeholders.

Neugebauer's claims challenging the validity of the bylaw remain alive and he will continue to pursue the truth through discovery and depositions, which he believes is necessary for good governance and to lower the company's cost of capital. priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder of Fermi America, delivered an investor presentation on June 30, 2026, expressing concerns about the company's contracts, including the EPC contract and gas contract, which he believes could be either assets or liabilities. Neugebauer emphasized the importance of having the right team to steward these contracts and ensure the company's success, and he advocated for a dual-track process and evidence-based decision-making in evaluating potential tenants. Neugebauer also stated that he and his team are not trying to come back to the company, but rather want to ensure that the board prioritizes the interests of shareholders.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)

priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer and other participants, including Vicksburg Investments Management LLC and Melissa A. Neugebauer 2020 Trust, have filed a definitive proxy statement with the SEC in connection with soliciting agent designations for a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders. The filing discloses that Mr. Neugebauer beneficially owns 146,516,035 shares of Fermi Inc.'s common stock, while the other participants do not beneficially own any shares. The participants are soliciting support for calling a special meeting of shareholders to be held as promptly as practicable.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest shareholder of Fermi Inc., has announced that the Texas Business Court granted his motion for expedited discovery, requiring Fermi to provide documentation and testimony regarding its recent actions, including the imposition of a 70% supermajority voting requirement. This development is a significant win for Neugebauer and Fermi's shareholders, who have been seeking greater accountability and transparency from the company's board. Neugebauer will also host a Town Hall webinar with analysts on June 30 to address questions and provide updates on his efforts to restore "good governance" to Fermi.

priority.summary: Toby R. Neugebauer, co-founder and largest individual shareholder of Fermi America, Inc., has proposed a town hall webinar for June 30th where he, Governor Rick Perry, and other parties will answer questions from analysts and journalists about the company's past, present, and future. Neugebauer is seeking to give shareholders a say in the company's direction, particularly regarding Project Matador, a 7,500-acre private power campus in Texas, and whether to pursue a strategic dual-track process to maximize shareholder value. The proposed webinar comes after Governor Perry and the board gave themselves a 70% supermajority vote threshold, making it difficult for shareholders to change course.

priority.title: SEC filing: DEFA14A — Fermi Inc. (SOLICITING MATERIAL PURSUANT TO SECTION 240.14A-12)
priority.formExplanation: Additional definitive proxy material from the company — its side of an ongoing solicitation (responses, letters to shareholders, talking points).
priority.summary: Fermi Inc is urging shareholders to sign and return the WHITE Consent Revocation Card in response to former CEO Toby Neugebauer's consent solicitation to call a Special Meeting of Shareholders, citing Neugebauer's pattern of false claims and inaccurate statements. Neugebauer is seeking to replace the Board and leadership team with his own nominees, which Fermi's Board believes would jeopardize shareholder value and benefit Neugebauer and his affiliates at the expense of other shareholders. The company is highlighting its commercial momentum and progress under new management, and warning shareholders not to sign or return any GREEN Consent Cards sent by Neugebauer.

priority.title: SEC filing: DFAN14A — Fermi Inc. (DEFINITIVE ADDITIONAL MATERIALS)
priority.formExplanation: Proxy-fight material from a non-management participant — solicitation filings made outside the company's own proxy (in a control contest, each side's letters and presentations land here).
priority.summary: Toby Neugebauer and his affiliated entities, collectively known as the Fermi Founder Parties, are soliciting shareholders to designate agents to call a special meeting of Fermi Inc. shareholders, with the goal of allowing shareholders to hear proposals and decide on the company's future. The Fermi Founder Parties, who collectively own 146,516,035 shares of Fermi Inc. common stock, are urging shareholders to consent to calling the special meeting by voting their GREEN voting instruction forms. Shareholders who own shares through a bank, broker, or other nominee can vote online, while those who hold shares in registered name must sign and return a physical GREEN agent designation card.

priority.excerpt: To own Fermi today, you need to believe Project Matador will convert its large permitted power platform and physical assets into long term tenant contracts, despite being pre revenue and reliant on external capital. In that context, Everson’s governance driven resignation and the US$375,000,000 convertible notes raise near term questions around board process, but do not obviously displace the core near term catalyst of securing binding tenant leases or the key risk of funding a multi billion dollar build.

The stake positions Nebius as a key participant in Nvidia’s broader AI ecosystem. Nebius Group sits at the intersection of cloud services and AI infrastructure, an area that continues to attract attention as demand for compute capacity, data processing, and model deployment tools grows.
